form.label: Using SPL Fields
form.description: How to use the spl_display and spl field types: what to put in your SPL rows (input) and how Form Viz renders them (output).

html.p: Form Viz has two SPL-aware field types. Each example below shows the input (the row your SPL returns) on the left and the output (how Form Viz renders it) on the right.

tr.td: No — display only, no input, no Submit
Editable multi-line SPL input with a Format button
User types it; saved as the answer
Yes — the typed SPL is written to KV Store

html.p: Both reuse the standard columns: question (heading/label), type, options, optional order, and optional description (markdown helper text shown above the field).

search.query: | makeresults | eval question="Reference detection SPL" | eval type="spl_display" | eval options="index=security sourcetype=wineventlog EventCode=4625 | stats count by src_ip user | where count > 5 | sort - count" | eval description="Read-only reference. Put the SPL in the **options** column; Form Viz auto-formats it (one command per pipe)." | eval order=1 | table options order question type description

html.p: Use this to show a query for reviewers to read. The SPL string goes in options. There is no input and no Submit button.

panel.title: Input — the row your SPL returns

search.query: | fields question type options order description
| table options order question type description

| makeresults | eval question="Containment SPL you ran" | eval type="spl" | eval options="" | eval description="Editable multi-line SPL box. Paste a query and click **Format SPL** to tidy it. The text is saved as the answer on submit." | eval order=1 | table options order question type description | append [ | makeresults | eval options="spl_guide_demo", order="", question="Form Name", type="form_name", description="" | table options order question type description ] | append [ | makeresults | eval options="form_responses", order="", question="Output Lookup", type="form_kv_output", description="" | table options order question type description ]

html.p: Use this when a user should submit SPL as their answer. Leave options empty. The rendered field is a monospace textarea with a Format SPL button.

panel.title: Input — the row your SPL returns

search.query: | search type!="form_name" type!="form_kv_output" | fields question type options order description

panel.title: Output — rendered by Form Viz (try the Format SPL button)

search.query: | table options order question type description

| makeresults count=3 | streamstats count AS row | eval order=row | eval question=case(row=1, "Search under review", row=2, "Is the SPL efficient?", row=3, "Proposed rewrite") | eval type=case(row=1, "spl_display", row=2, "radio", row=3, "spl") | eval options=case( row=1, "index=web status>=500 | stats count by uri_path | sort - count", row=2, "yes,no,partial", row=3, "") | eval description=case( row=1, "Reference only — the query being reviewed.", row=2, "Answer based on the reference above.", row=3, "Optional: paste a cleaner version and click Format SPL.") | table options order question type description | append [ | makeresults | eval options="spl_guide_demo", order="", question="Form Name", type="form_name", description="" | table options order question type description ] | append [ | makeresults | eval options="form_responses", order="", question="Output Lookup", type="form_kv_output", description="" | table options order question type description ]

html.p: A read-only spl_display reference, a radio question, and an editable spl answer in one form. Submit writes only the answered input rows (the spl_display row is never saved).

panel.title: Input — the rows your SPL returns

search.query: | search type!="form_name" type!="form_kv_output" | fields question type options order description
| table options order question type description

html.p: Demo submits above use form name spl_guide_demo. Each submission batch shares a submission_id, so you can tell submissions apart even when the form name repeats.

search.query: | inputlookup form_responses | search form_name="spl_guide_demo" | sort -_time +order | table timestamp, submission_id, user, form_name, order, question, answer
